name: Dark photon kinetic-mixing portal
summary: Adds a hidden-sector U(1)' gauge boson (the dark photon A') that mixes with SM hypercharge through a renormalisable kinetic-mixing term -(epsilon/2) F^{Y mu nu} F'_{mu nu}. The dark photon mediates a small SM-hidden-sector coupling proportional to epsilon ~ 10^-9 - 10^-3, providing a generic portal to a dark sector that may itself host dark-matter candidates.

addresses_puzzles.note: Doesn't pick a DM candidate by itself but supplies the renormalisable portal between SM and a dark sector that does. Massive A' itself can be DM (longitudinal mode produced via inflationary fluctuations); fermionic chi charged under U(1)' can be the DM in 'dark sector freeze-out / freeze-in' models.

touches_benchmarks.note: Direct target of NA64, BaBar, Belle II, FASER and SHiP searches in the (m_A', epsilon) plane. Current bound epsilon < 10^-3 for m_A' ~ MeV-GeV.
If chi is the DM and couples via A' exchange, LZ bounds constrain g_chi^2 epsilon^2 / m_A'^4.

new_fields: dark photon A' (massive U(1)' gauge boson)
introduces.note: Kinetic mixing parameter epsilon and dark-photon mass m_A'. Dark-sector content (DM mass, couplings) adds further parameters when specified.

history: Holdom (1986), 'Two U(1)'s and epsilon charge shifts', Phys. Lett. B 166, 196. Revived in the 2000s as the simplest renormalisable hidden-sector portal; extensive low-energy and beam-dump experimental program 2010s-present.

references.citation: Holdom, 'Two U(1)\'s and epsilon charge shifts', Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (1986)
Pospelov, 'Secluded U(1) below the weak scale', Phys. Rev. D 80, 095002 (2009)
